article.body_preview:
DHAKA, June 7, 2023 (BSS) - Federation of Bangladesh Chambers of Commerce and Industry (FBCCI) President Md Jashim Uddin today urged the government to extend policy support towards import-substitute industries in the country to lessen import pressure on foreign exchange.

The FBCCI president made the call while addressing a post-budget discussion as a special guest organized by the Economic Reporters' Forum (ERF) jointly with RAPID and Asia Foundation held at the ERF auditorium at Paltan in the

llm_connections.evidence_quote: So, the Bangladesh Bank's move only to cut imports is not a solution to reduce stress on the dollar,
llm_connections.scope_justification: The quote explicitly links a national policy (cutting imports) to 'stress on the dollar', referring to country-level USD/BDT exchange-rate pressure, which matches the feature's exchange-rate volatility scope.

verification.quote_best_match: "So, the Bangladesh Bank's move only to cut imports is not a solution to reduce stress on the dollar,"
verification.judge_reasoning: The exact quote appears in the article. It explicitly states that cutting imports 'is not a solution to reduce stress on the dollar,' asserting a causal claim that the import-cutting policy fails to reduce exchange-rate pressure, and the context (Bangladesh Bank, Taka per dollar) is at the national currency level.
